channel.title: Missing-Property on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Missing-Property on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: [Solution] Groovy MissingPropertyException
item.description:
What This Error Means

A MissingPropertyException is thrown when Groovy tries to access a property that doesn’t exist on an object or in the current binding/scope.

Common Causes

  • Typo in property name
  • Property not defined in the current scope
  • Accessing a property on a wrong object type
  • Closures not having access to outer scope variables
  • Script binding issues

How to Fix

// WRONG: Typo in property name
def person = [name: "Alice", age: 30]
println person.nme // MissingPropertyException

// CORRECT: Use correct property name
println person.name

// WRONG: Property not defined
class Foo {
 void bar() { println x } // x not defined
}

// CORRECT: Define the property
class Foo {
 def x = 42
 void bar() { println x }
}

// WRONG: Closure missing outer variable
def localVar = 10
def cl = { println localVar }
localVar = null // Now closure fails
cl() // May fail

// CORRECT: Capture value
def localVar = 10
def captured = localVar
def cl = { println captured }
cl() // Works

Examples

// Example 1: Check property existence
def person = [name: "Alice", age: 30]
println person.containsKey("name") // true
println person.containsKey("email") // false

// Example 2: Use propertyMissing for dynamic properties
class DynamicProps {
 def props = [:]
 def propertyMissing(String name) {
 props[name]
 }
 def propertyMissing(String name, value) {
 props[name] = value
 }
}

// Example 3: Safe navigation
def obj = null
println obj?.someProperty // null, no exception

Related Errors

  • groovy-missingmethod — missing method
  • groovy-nullpointererror — null pointer
  • groovy-metaclasserror — metaclass error

item.title: [Solution] TypeScript Missing Property in Type — Structural Type Error Fix
item.description:
TypeScript: Property ‘X’ is missing in type

This error occurs when an object literal is missing a property that the target type requires. TypeScript’s structural type system requires that every required property in the target interface is present in the source type.

Common Causes

  • Missing required interface properties — not all fields of an interface are provided
  • Function return type mismatch — a function returns an object missing required fields
  • Spreading incomplete objects — spreading an object that doesn’t have all required properties
  • API response typing — typing a response more strictly than the actual data

How to Fix

interface User {
 id: number;
 name: string;
 email: string;
 role: "admin" | "user";
}

// Cause 1: Missing properties in object literal
const user: User = {
 id: 1,
 name: "Alice",
 // TS2741: Property 'email' is missing
};

// Fix: provide all required properties
const user: User = {
 id: 1,
 name: "Alice",
 email: "alice@example.com",
 role: "user",
};

// Cause 2: Partial object from a function
function getUser(): User {
 return { id: 1, name: "Alice" }; // TS2741: missing email and role
}

// Fix: ensure function returns all required fields
function getUser(): User {
 return { id: 1, name: "Alice", email: "alice@example.com", role: "user" };
}

// Cause 3: Make properties optional if they aren't always needed
interface User {
 id: number;
 name: string;
 email?: string; // optional
 role?: "admin" | "user"; // optional
}

Examples

interface Product {
 id: number;
 name: string;
 price: number;
 description: string;
}

// Spread incomplete object
const partial = { id: 1, name: "Widget" };
const product: Product = { ...partial, price: 9.99 }; // TS2741: missing description

// Fix: include all fields
const product: Product = { ...partial, price: 9.99, description: "A widget" };

// Array of objects — one is missing a field
const items: Product[] = [
 { id: 1, name: "A", price: 10, description: "desc" },
 { id: 2, name: "B", price: 20 }, // TS2741: missing description
];

Related Errors

  • TS2322: Type not assignable — broader assignability issues
  • TS2339: Property does not exist on type — accessing extra/wrong properties
  • TS2740: Type missing properties — related variant for class types

item.title: [Solution] TypeScript TS2339 — Property 'X' does not exist on type 'Y'
item.description:
TS2339 — Property ‘X’ does not exist on type ‘Y’

TS2339 occurs when you try to access a property on a type that does not define it. TypeScript’s type system enforces that you can only access properties that exist on the declared type.

Common Causes

// Cause 1: Accessing non-existent property
interface User {
 name: string;
 age: number;
}
const user: User = { name: "Alice", age: 30 };
console.log(user.email); // TS2339

// Cause 2: Wrong type annotation
const data: string = "hello";
data.length; // OK
data.toFixed(2); // TS2339: string has no toFixed

// Cause 3: Using API response without type definition
const response = fetch("/api/user");
const data = await response.json();
console.log(data.user.name); // TS2339: 'user' does not exist

How to Fix

Fix 1: Add missing property to interface

interface User {
 name: string;
 age: number;
 email: string;
}

Fix 2: Use optional chaining

console.log(user?.email);

Fix 3: Type the API response

interface ApiResponse {
 user: { name: string; age: number };
}
const data: ApiResponse = await response.json();
console.log(data.user.name);

Fix 4: Use type assertion or index signature

interface StringMap {
 [key: string]: string;
}
const map: StringMap = { name: "Alice" };
console.log(map["name"]);

Related Errors

  • TS2339: Property is type-only import — type-only import access.
  • TS2551: Property does not exist - did you mean — typo suggestions.
  • TS2741: Property is missing in type — missing required property.

item.title: [Solution] TypeScript TS2741 — Property Is Missing in Type
item.description:
TS2741 — Property ‘X’ is missing in type ‘Y’

TS2741 occurs when an object literal or value is missing required properties that the target type demands. This is a specific variant of TS2322 focused on missing properties in object shapes.

Common Causes

// Cause 1: Missing required property in object literal
interface User {
 name: string;
 age: number;
 email: string;
}
const user: User = { name: "Alice", age: 30 }; // TS2741: Property 'email' is missing

// Cause 2: Missing property in function return
interface Config {
 host: string;
 port: number;
 debug: boolean;
}
function getConfig(): Config {
 return { host: "localhost", port: 3000 }; // TS2741: 'debug' is missing
}

// Cause 3: Missing property when implementing an interface
interface Serializable {
 serialize(): string;
 validate(): boolean;
}
class MyClass implements Serializable {
 serialize() { return ""; } // TS2741: 'validate' is missing
}

// Cause 4: Missing property in array of objects
const users: User[] = [
 { name: "Alice", age: 30 }, // TS2741: 'email' is missing
];

Solutions

Fix 1: Add all required properties

// Wrong
const user: User = { name: "Alice", age: 30 };

// Correct
const user: User = { name: "Alice", age: 30, email: "alice@example.com" };

Fix 2: Make optional properties with ?

// If email isn't always needed, mark it optional
interface User {
 name: string;
 age: number;
 email?: string; // Optional
}
const user: User = { name: "Alice", age: 30 }; // OK

Fix 3: Use default values or spread

function getConfig(): Config {
 const defaults = { debug: false };
 return { ...defaults, host: "localhost", port: 3000 };
}

Fix 4: Implement all interface members

class MyClass implements Serializable {
 serialize() { return ""; }
 validate() { return true; } // Implement missing method
}

Examples

// Example 1: React component props
interface ButtonProps {
 onClick: () => void;
 label: string;
 variant: "primary" | "secondary";
}
// Missing 'variant' prop
<Button onClick={handler} label="Click" />; // TS2741

// Example 2: API response type
interface ApiResponse {
 status: number;
 data: unknown;
 timestamp: string;
}
const res: ApiResponse = { status: 200, data: {} }; // TS2741

Related Errors

  • TS2322: Type is not assignable — broader assignability including extra properties.
  • TS2339: Property does not exist on type — accessing non-existent properties.
  • TS2749: Refers to a value, used as a type — value vs type confusion.
